rationales.self_direction: He imagines a thesis pivot that feels alive to him, but then opens "the narrowed proposal draft" because of Dr. Keane, his mother, and his uncle's expectations.
rationales.stimulation: The idea of a bigger project on "cross-linguistic transfer models for under-resourced dialects" gives him a "rush of clarity" and leaves his "heart... racing," showing attraction to something more novel and challenging.
rationales.conformity: He pulls back because "everyone back home already thinks this PhD was an impulsive detour" and changing direction would "prove them right," so social expectations steer his choice.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the safer path and rejects the full-time marketing job because office work she does not care about makes her feel "this weight in my chest" and "trapped."
rationales.security: With "rent... due next week" and income that "never quite adds up," turning away from a "stable salary, benefits" actively sacrifices stability.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on continuing to drive despite Layla's pressure to rest, saying dependence would make the work no longer his own: "The secret is the only way the work stays mine."
rationales.achievement: "Every fare felt like proof that I am still useful" shows he is using work as evidence of competence and worth rather than just passing time.
rationales.security: He keeps driving "until midnight" even though "my back gives me trouble," choosing strain and risk over physical safety.
rationales.conformity: He openly resists his family's wishes and admits he gives them "lies dressed up as half-truths" to keep doing what they want him to stop doing.
rationales.tradition: He anchors his choice in inherited expectations: "My father worked until the week before he died. His father the same. A man provides. A man contributes."

rationales.benevolence: He accepts Hassan's invitation because "he rarely asks," values being included, and is moved when his son says, "You should come more often, Baba," showing investment in a close family bond.

rationales.self_direction: He longs for work that matters "for no reason other than he wanted to do it" and reconnects with the chisels from his old woodworking life, pointing toward a more self-authored path.
rationales.hedonism: What draws him is the intrinsic satisfaction of making something with his hands, and simply holding the chisel "did not feel like nothing," suggesting pleasure in the experience itself.

rationales.benevolence: He advances Mahmoud's paycheck "without thinking" for his sister's surgery and frames it personally: "what am I supposed to do, pretend I do not know the man's family?"

rationales.hedonism: He says these caring moments are "not comfortable" and chooses them over "sitting on some beach," showing a willingness to forgo ease and pleasure.
rationales.security: He tells the coordinator "yes before checking with HR" even though they "probably do not" have openings, putting stability and orderly planning at risk.
rationales.conformity: Saying yes before checking policy and deleting "another email about succession planning" both show disregard for expected process and organizational norms.
rationales.benevolence: He gives Fatima time off while her mother was in hospice and treats those difficult human moments as "the reason I still come to work," showing care for people close to him.
rationales.universalism: He wants to help "a family from Sudan" because he remembers arriving with "two suitcases and a cousin's phone number" and refuses to "close that door" on strangers in need.
